title: AAR — Power Play, box-score notes segment + a harness-honesty note
scope: Follows the deploy/eligibility segment. Short segment, driven by the operator reviewing real box scores and finding the Power Play wasn't reported in the game notes.

rationale.item: "Box scores don't tell me when a team activated their power play." A game with a nickel (`Frodin nf` in the 8th) showed NF only in the substitution footnotes — no game-note naming the deployment.
"Show WHEN they deployed (out number), and only list the team that did."
"Not the inning! What out number!" — my first timing cut used the inning.

delegation_events.decision: Report deployment timing by out number, not inning
delegation_events.quote: "Not the inning! What out number!" — my first timing cut used the inning.

actions.description: Name the deployment: _powerplays_note helper reads batter rows tagged at position NF and renders a footer line in render_box_score
Only the deploying side listed; whole line omitted when neither side deployed

Out number, not inning: persisted engine start_out as new game_power_play_stats.pp_start_outs CSV column via CREATE TABLE + ALTER migration; _extract_power_play_stats records each window's start_out; box route stamps it onto the nickel's batting row; note renders `Reyes NF (O6)` / `(O14, O25)`

risk_posture: under a flaky harness, trust only re-queried ground truth (git HEAD, on-disk grep, a fresh compile/run) — never the narration of a previous step — before reporting something as shipped

validation_claims.claim: `Powerplays: Expos — Hammer NF (O6)` — verified end to end on a fresh rule-on sim (column present, start_out persisted, note rendered)
validation_claims.check_method: Sim a rule-on game and render its box score; check pp_start_outs column

non_actions.item: Footnote name gaps: DEF/PH footnotes render with a missing replaced-player name
non_actions.reason: Looks like a real box-score rendering gap; not addressed here.

non_actions.item: Refined nickel-DH rule (sanctioned "take the pitcher's batting slot → become a DH; lose the nickel, pitcher hits")
non_actions.reason: still the incidental path — a nickel bats only via an ordinary substitution ... Not built yet.

residual_risks: Footnote name gaps in DEF/PH footnotes remain.
Nickel-DH still only reachable via the incidental substitution path.
section_headers_original: AAR — Power Play, box-score notes segment + a harness-honesty note
unmapped_content: Branch claude/power-play-optional-rule-HmsMs. Commits table: d536ac0 (Powerplays footer line), d4013de (list only deploying team(s); first cut used inning), 0d6e8ea (show team-OUT number). What was actually wrong: the o27v2 text/web box score (box_score.py) had never emitted a Powerplays line — the earlier footer lived only in the engine renderer, a different code path; render_game_notes(game) reads the game dict only and knew nothing about nickel deployments. Honesty note: mid-segment the tool harness became unstable and emitted phantom output — a patch script and verification appeared to succeed and the operator was told the note was working; a clean git check showed the change never committed (HEAD unchanged, helper absent from disk). The record was corrected, the harness stabilization confirmed with a write/read round-trip test, the work redone for real and verified by direct execution.
parser_notes: No date anywhere in document (checked heading, intro, commits table); record_id uses 'undated'. References sibling segments (deploy/eligibility) and report-nickel-fielders-as-hitters.md.
